summary: Backend: 5/5 pytest tests PASS via public URL (health GET /api/, POST /api/ocr/pdf happy-path with synthetic 3-row PDF returning HH:MM 24h times, POST /api/ocr/pdf empty body → 422 graceful, POST /api/ocr/pdf invalid base64 → 502 JSON with 'detail' (no Python crash), POST /api/ocr/timetable regression). Frontend: All requested flows PASS on mobile viewport (390x844). Settings → manual-edit-btn navigates to /editor showing 'Create a blank month'. create-days-30 renders 30 day-chips + all med-<prayer>-start/jamaat + med-sunrise + editor-save-btn. Typed 04:15 into med-fajr-start and 04:35 into med-fajr-jamaat, switched to day-chip-5 (values blank as expected) then back to day-chip-1 (values persisted). editor-save-btn shows 'Saved!' toast. Upload tab (/upload) shows pick-camera-btn, pick-gallery-btn, pick-pdf-btn, pick-csv-btn, manual-editor-link — link navigates to /editor. Home clock/hijri, tab bar, and Settings toggles (24h/theme/sunrise) all render and respond. No red screens, no console errors (only known expo web warnings about boxShadow/pointerEvents/expo-notifications on web).

minor.issue: Invalid base64 payload (padding error) is caught by the broad `except Exception` block and re-raised as HTTPException(502) → 'PDF OCR failed: Incorrect padding'. Backend does NOT crash and JSON is well-formed, but 502 is semantically incorrect for a client input error and Cloudflare rewrites any origin 5xx into its own HTML 502 page, so the frontend loses the JSON 'detail'. Recommend catching binascii.Error explicitly and returning 400/422 with 'Invalid base64 file_base64'.

issues: Console warnings: 'shadow*' style props deprecated (use boxShadow) and 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated' — RN Web deprecations only, no visual impact but should be cleaned up eventually in shared components.

action_items: (MINOR) In /app/backend/server.py ocr_pdf: wrap `b64lib.b64decode(data)` in its own try/except binascii.Error → raise HTTPException(400, 'Invalid base64 payload'). Currently invalid base64 leaks through the generic `except Exception` and becomes a 502, which Cloudflare then rewrites to an HTML error page, hiding the JSON error from the mobile UI.
(OPTIONAL) Address RN-Web deprecation warnings ('shadow*' style props → boxShadow; props.pointerEvents → style.pointerEvents) in shared components/theme to future-proof.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py PDF endpoint correctly writes/cleans up the temp file in finally.
server.py maps the invalid-base64 case to 502 which is misleading (should be 4xx).
editor.tsx correctly uses KeyboardAvoidingView, testIDs on every interactive element, and Text-wrapped strings. State persists across chip taps.
upload.tsx renders four import buttons and manual-editor-link with correct testIDs and navigates via expo-router push('/editor').
seed_data_creation: Synthetic in-memory PDF timetable generated via PIL (test_pdf_ocr.py :: _make_timetable_pdf_b64) with 3 rows (Aug 1-3 2025). Each pytest run also generates the regression JPEG via gen_timetable_image. One document per successful OCR call is inserted into MongoDB db.timetables (marked source='pdf' for the PDF endpoint). No cleanup — acceptable for MVP.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL: https://salah-alarm-6.preview.emergentagent.com. Frontend .env key is EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (not EXPO_BACKEND_URL). Emergent LLM key rate-limits concurrent calls — keep pytest sequential (no -n xdist). Backend PDF endpoint uses FileContentWithMimeType with gemini-3.1-pro-preview and takes ~10-20s per PDF. The /editor route persists per-day edits in local state via rowIdx switching. If testing invalid-base64 via public URL, expect Cloudflare 502 HTML — hit http://localhost:8001 directly to see the actual FastAPI JSON error body.
rca_of_issue: MINOR — POST /api/ocr/pdf with invalid base64 returns 502 instead of 4xx. Root cause: `raw_bytes = b64lib.b64decode(data)` at server.py:154 raises binascii.Error which is caught by the broad `except Exception` at :172 and mapped to HTTPException(502). Because Cloudflare's edge rewrites any origin 5xx into its own HTML 502 page, the frontend never sees the JSON 'detail'. Mitigation: add `try: raw_bytes = b64lib.b64decode(data) \n except binascii.Error: raise HTTPException(400, 'Invalid base64 payload')` before the LLM call. Empty body already correctly returns 422 via Pydantic.
